data.title: NatWest receives regulatory notice over breach of rules related to charges, disclosure
data.description:
UK-based NatWest Group (NYSE:NWG) announced on Wednesday the receipt of a regulatory notice over breach of rules related to charges levied on customers, disclosure of costs on overdrafts and loans, and the offering of a price and eligibility tool to clients.

NWG shares were -0.35% pre-market to $14.44.

The bank failed to provide the correct monthly maximum charge to about 104,800 customers on unarranged overdrafts, according to the statement by the UK Competition and Markets Authority.

NatWest failed to include the representative cost in equivalent annual rate terms of their overdrafts in its letters to 66,765 SME customers, which included an offer to renew an overdraft between May 2021 and February 2024.

Additionally, the Edinburgh-based lender could not continuously offer the price and eligibility tool, which enables SMEs to obtain an indicative price quote and indication of their eligibility for unsecured loans and standard tariff unsecured business overdrafts.

data.title: reAlpha appoints Mike Logozzo as CEO
data.description:
• reAlpha Tech (NASDAQ:AIRE) announced Wednesday that President and COO Mike Logozzo has been appointed CEO effective June 3, 2025.
  • Founder and former CEO Giri Devanur will now serve as Executive Chairman of the Board.
  • Logozzo will also act as Interim COO until a successor is named.

data.title: Stock index futures give up gains as job data comes in cooler than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures pared their earlier gains on Wednesday as the private sector job growth cooled more than consensus in May.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were flat, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 5 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.94%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP. The pace of private hiring in May reached its lowest level since March 2023.

U.S. major averages ended Tuesday with solid gains as encouraging job openings data overshadowed a major intergovernmental organization downgrading its global and U.S. economic growth projections.

In trade news, Donald Trump said that Chinese President Xi Jinping has been “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, as the White House hinted at the possibility of the two leaders speaking this week amid escalating trade tensions.

"The mood was boosted yesterday by tech and semiconductor stocks, as Meta’s deal to enter a 20-year agreement with the largest U.S. nuclear operator seemed to energize the sector. The deal was said to be cheaper than a similar one powered by Microsoft last year, as it used existing facilities," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

One of the most important catalysts for the recovery was that JOLTS report, which showed the US labor market in a more robust position than previously thought, Reid added. 

Economists, on average, expect it to rise to 111K.

The May S&P Global Composite PMI is forecasted to come in at 52.1, while the ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI is expected to come in at 52.0.

data.title: U.S. private sector job growth cools more than consensus in May: ADP jobs report
data.description:
U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday. 

"After a strong start to the year, hiring is losing momentum," said Dr. Nela Richardson, chief economist, ADP. "Pay growth, however, was little changed in May, holding at robust levels for both job-stayers and job-changers."

Annual pay increased 4.5% Y/Y for those who stayed in their jobs, flat from the 4.5% Y/Y rise in April. Job changers, meanwhile, saw their annual pay increase accelerate to 6.9% from 6.7% in the previous month.

By sector, education and health services lost 13 jobs and professional/business services jobs dropped by 17K. Meanwhile, leisure/hospitality boosted rolls by 38K and financial activities added 20K.

By region, the West added 37K jobs, while the Northeast lost 19K.

data.title: FangDD to implement 1-for-16 share consolidation effective June 9
data.description:
• Fangdd Network (NASDAQ:DUO) will conduct a 1-for-16 share consolidation effective 8:00 a.m. ET on June 9, 2025.
  • The move aims to restore compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.
  • Outstanding shares will be reduced from 62.47M to 3.90M as a result of the reverse split.
  • DUO shares fall 4% premarket on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Digimarc announces COO appointment
data.description:
• Digimarc (NASDAQ:DMRC) has promoted Carle Quinn to chief operating officer, effective immediately.
  • Quinn joined Digimarc as chief people officer in April 2024.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: REV Group rallies after posting a beat-and-raise quarter
data.description:
REV Group (NYSE:REVG) tracked higher in early action on Wednesday after the Wisconsin-based company beat FQ2 earnings expectations.

Revenue was up 2.0% year-over-year to $629.1 million. Excluding the impact of the Bus Manufacturing Businesses, sales increased 7.7% compared to the prior year quarter. The increase was primarily due to higher net sales in the Specialty Vehicles segment, partially offset by lower net sales in the Recreational Vehicles segment.

Excluding the impact of the Bus Manufacturing Businesses, adjusted EBITDA was up 63.6% compared to a year ago. The increase was primarily due to the higher contribution from the Specialty Vehicles segment and lower contribution from the Recreational Vehicles segment.

"The standout this quarter was the sustained year-over-year increase in manufacturing throughput within the fire group, which played a pivotal role in driving our top-line growth," stated REV Group (NYSE:REVG) CEO, Mark Skonieczny. "Within the quarter we utilized our robust cash flow and financial position to repurchase $88 million of shares, which we view as an attractive use of capital. Building on the performance demonstrated through the first half of the fiscal year, we also plan to accelerate certain capital investments to further our manufacturing throughput goals including a $20 million investment in our Brandon, South Dakota location," he added.

REV Group (REVG) raises guidance for FY25, now seeing FY25 revenue of $2.35 billion to $2.45 billion (midpoint $2.40 billion), vs. $2.38 billion consensus and up from prior guidance of $2.30 billion to $2.40 billion.

Shares of REV Group (REVG) rallied 5.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday, after gaining 1.9% on Tuesday.

data.title: Old Dominion Freight Line reports decline in May revenue per day
data.description:
Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ:ODFL) announced on Wednesday a decline in its revenue per day for the month of May on the back of a continued softness in the economy and lower fuel prices.

Revenue per day decreased 5.8% year-over-year in May on an 8.4% decline in LTL tons per day. The decline was attributed to the fall of 6.8% in LTL shipments per day and 1.9% in LTL weight per shipment.

"Our revenue results for May reflect continued softness in the domestic economy as well as the impact of lower fuel prices on our yields," said the cargo transportation company's CEO Marty Freeman.

Shares were +0.19% pre-market to $163.06.

data.title: Juggernaut Exploration secures $1.1M financing to advance Big One gold-silver project
data.description:
• Juggernaut Exploration (TSXV:JUGR:CA) has fully subscribed a $1.1M hard dollar financing round.
  • The funding supports exploration at the Big One property in BC’s Golden Triangle, near Newmont’s Galore Creek.
  • The project includes a newly discovered 11 km “Highway of Gold” around the Eldorado porphyry system.
  • Assays from the Big One discovery returned up to 79.01 g/t gold and 3,157.89 g/t silver from over 200 veins.
  • The financing includes 1.72M units at $0.64 each, with a warrant exercisable at $0.84 for 60 months.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • Financing is expected to close on June 13, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: RFK Jr. says Moderna agreed to another clinical trial to support new COVID shot
data.description:
Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) has agreed to conduct another placebo-controlled clinical trial for its newly approved next-gen COVID-19 vaccine, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has said.

Late last week, the FDA approved the messenger RNA-based shot branded as mNEXSPIKE for all adults aged 65 and above, as well as those aged 12–64 years with at least one underlying risk factor.

The approval was based on data from an active-controlled Phase 3 clinical trial in which mNEXSPIKE generated a higher immune response against COVID-19 compared to Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) original COVID shot, Spikevax.

“I want to address those of you who have anxieties about FDA's limited approval of a new mRNA COVID vaccine for high-risk populations,” RFK Jr. said in an X post on Tuesday, adding, “Moderna has agreed to a true placebo-controlled trial of the new vaccine.”

“The FDA will monitor and collect data throughout the trial for every adverse outcome—not just a table list of expected outcomes,” he noted, adding, “The FDA will scrutinize every aspect of the trial.” Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) was not immediately available for Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.
